items.prompt: Why can uncertainty sampling alone be problematic?
choices: It may over-focus on difficult or ambiguous outliers and skew the labeled set
It requires labeling every item in the pool
It only works for regression, never classification
items.explain: Section 11.3 notes uncertainty sampling can over-weight hard cases and miss distribution coverage.

items.prompt: Example 11.5 queues an X-ray scored 0.52 abnormal and 0.48 normal because of the near-tie.
items.explain: Least-confidence selection targets near decision-boundary ties for expert labels.

items.prompt: What does diversity-based sampling primarily ensure?
choices: Selected samples cover a wide range of the data distribution
Only the single hardest outlier is labeled each round
All labels come from distant databases
items.explain: Diversity sampling, often via clustering, prevents overfitting to a narrow uncertain subset.

items.prompt: In Example 11.7, how does cost-sensitive routing allocate legal labeling effort?
items.rubric: Ambiguous contracts to experts; simpler documents to non-experts / maximize gain per dollar.
items.sample_answer: Ambiguous contracts go to legal experts; simpler documents go to non-experts to maximize accuracy gain per dollar.

items.prompt: In Example 11.8, experts review every X-ray tumor proposal, not only ambiguous cases.
items.explain: Experts review ambiguous cases; corrections retrain so later rounds need fewer edits.
